stacks.title: Cinematic Frame Sequence
stacks.summary: A generated or rendered hero video is converted into a scroll-scrubbed frame sequence, then layered with GSAP pinned timelines and code-native UI overlays.
use_when: The hero needs exact art-directed scroll states.
The main visual is a transformation that video generation, offline rendering, or pre-rendered frames can control better than live WebGL.
The page needs a Terminal-style cinematic sequence where one object or world carries the story.
avoid_when: The visual needs true user-controlled 3D inspection.
The animation is simple enough for CSS or Motion.
The project cannot afford mobile frame assets, posters, reduced-motion fallbacks, and load checks.

tools.name: ffmpeg
tools.purpose: Extract scroll frames, posters, and optimized support video.

tools.name: GSAP ScrollTrigger
tools.purpose: Pin sections, scrub timelines, coordinate overlays, and expose scroll progress.

tools.name: generated video model
tools.purpose: Create the hero transformation or support loops when real media does not exist.

tools.name: HTML/CSS overlays
tools.purpose: Keep labels, CTAs, HUDs, proof, and responsive text accessible.

sequence: Preload enough frames for the first scroll segment.
Map scroll progress to phase and frame index.
Use GSAP ScrollTrigger for pinning, scrub, and overlay timelines when the project uses GSAP.
Render copy, CTA, and HUD overlays in HTML.
Pause, reduce, or replace motion when reduced-motion is enabled.

video_prompt_shape: [domain world/object], [camera motion], [transformation over time], [material and lighting], [enterprise or editorial tone], [composition constraints], no readable text, no logos, clean loop or scroll-scrub friendly motion, high detail, stable geometry
fallbacks: HTML text overlays that still render without motion
stable final CTA section outside the heavy pinned sequence
qa_checks: Desktop and mobile screenshots at narrative checkpoints.
All frame, poster, and video URLs return 200.
Reduced-motion path renders without scroll-scrub dependency.
Text does not overlap at mobile widths.
Scroll checkpoints map to intended phase and frame.
gotchas: Do not bake readable text or logos into generated video.
Do not extract only one frame per second for production-smooth scrub unless the design intentionally wants a stepped storyboard.
Do not use live WebGL when a pre-rendered frame sequence gives better art direction and easier debugging.
Do not ship without a poster, mobile path, and reduced-motion fallback.

reference_examples.label: Terminal Industries
reference_examples.notes: Observed pre-rendered media and frame-sequence-heavy scroll hero.

reference_examples.label: Nomous Agency Landing Page
reference_examples.notes: Private sticky-scroll prototype with phase constants, RAF scrubbing, and video readiness handling.
